vulnerabilities.recommended_fix: Use parameterized queries. Apply least-privilege DB roles. Disable stacked queries if possible. Use parameterized queries. Restrict DELETE permissions. Enable soft-delete patterns. Use parameterized queries. NEVER grant DROP privileges to application database users. Use parameterized queries. Validate all input server-side before insertion.

ddos_vulnerabilities.observation: Server accepted 200 requests within 0.8s without throttling. Avg response: 74ms, baseline: 32ms, errors: 0/200.
ddos_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement request rate limiting or WAF protection.

ddos_vulnerabilities.observation: Server accepted 40/40 partial HTTP connections without enforcing connection timeouts. 40 connections remained alive after slow header injection.
ddos_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement connection timeouts, limit concurrent connections per IP, or deploy a reverse proxy with Slowloris protection.

ddos_vulnerabilities.observation: Server accepted a 10MB POST payload (status 405) without enforcing size limits.
ddos_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Enforce request body size limits (e.g., 1–5MB max) at the web server or reverse proxy level.

ddos_vulnerabilities.observation: Server accepted 100/100 login attempts without rate limiting or account lockout. Only 0 were blocked.
ddos_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement login rate limiting, account lockout after failed attempts, CAPTCHA after repeated failures, and IP-based throttling.

ddos_vulnerabilities.observation: Server accepted 30/30 file uploads without enforcing upload frequency or size restrictions.
ddos_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Enforce upload rate limits, maximum file size, disk quota per user, and file type validation.

ddos_vulnerabilities.observation: Server recomputes responses for cache-busted requests. Avg cached: 32ms, avg busted: 60ms (difference: 28ms). An attacker could send requests with random parameters to bypass caching.
ddos_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement cache key normalization to ignore unknown query parameters, or use a CDN with aggressive caching rules.

ddos_vulnerabilities.observation: Rate limiting was bypassed using spoofed X-Forwarded-For headers. 50/50 requests succeeded.
ddos_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Do not trust client-supplied headers (X-Forwarded-For) for rate limiting. Use the actual connection IP from the socket layer, or a trusted proxy header configured at the infrastructure level.

verdicts.observation: Server accepted 200 requests within 0.8s without throttling. Avg response: 74ms, baseline: 32ms, errors: 0/200.
verdicts.recommendation: Implement request rate limiting or WAF protection.
verdicts.explanation: Repeated malicious behaviour detected from source IP. IP temporarily blocked for 15 minutes.

verdicts.observation: Server accepted 40/40 partial HTTP connections without enforcing connection timeouts. 40 connections remained alive after slow header injection.
verdicts.recommendation: Implement connection timeouts, limit concurrent connections per IP, or deploy a reverse proxy with Slowloris protection.
verdicts.explanation: Excessive open connections detected. Concurrent connection limit enforced: max 10 per IP, idle timeout 30s.

verdicts.observation: Server accepted a 10MB POST payload (status 405) without enforcing size limits.
verdicts.recommendation: Enforce request body size limits (e.g., 1–5MB max) at the web server or reverse proxy level.
verdicts.explanation: Abnormally large request payload detected. Requests exceeding 10MB rejected (HTTP 413).

verdicts.observation: Server recomputes responses for cache-busted requests. Avg cached: 32ms, avg busted: 60ms (difference: 28ms). An attacker could send requests with random parameters to bypass caching.

verdicts.observation: Rate limiting was bypassed using spoofed X-Forwarded-For headers. 50/50 requests succeeded.
verdicts.recommendation: Do not trust client-supplied headers (X-Forwarded-For) for rate limiting. Use the actual connection IP from the socket layer, or a trusted proxy header configured at the infrastructure level.
verdicts.explanation: Rate limit evasion attempt detected (rotating headers/user-agents). Adaptive fingerprint-based rate limiting engaged.
